heading: Step 3: Create a  index.
body:
Run the [atlas clusters search indexes create](atlas/cli/current/command/atlas-clusters-search-indexes-create)
command in your terminal, replacing `<clusterName>` with the
name of the cluster, and `<path-to-file>` with the
path to the `search-index.json` file:

```shell
atlas clusters search indexes create --clusterName <cluster-name> --file <path-to-file>

```

The MongoDB Search dateFacet type takes the following parameters:

| Option | Type | Necessity | Description                                                               |
| ------ | ---- | --------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| type   | uuid | Required  | Human-readable label that identifies this field type. Value must be uuid. |

Use [Universally Unique Identifiers](manual/reference/method/UUID) (UUIDs) when you need values that are virtually guaranteed to be unique.
You can use the MongoDB Search `uuid` type to index, query, and [sorting](sort-ref) over
[BSON Binary Subtype 4](manual/reference/bson-types) fields.
